title: Resolve symbolic release identity in supply-chain evidence
outOfScope: Changing release distribution semantics or allowing HEAD.

riskAssessment.reason: Supply-chain evidence must compare symbolic candidate identity without weakening concrete publication evidence.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized root-cause process correction before publication.

preReviewWarnings: Replace with task-specific review focus, or clear when no special review focus remains.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Release supply-chain comparison resolves controlled origin identity refs to concrete SHA before comparing computed evidence.
HEAD and unsupported symbolic refs remain rejected; concrete identity behavior remains unchanged.
Focused tests, full Finish/archive/PR/merge/ai-close lifecycle complete without publication.
guidelines: Preserve candidate versus published evidence distinction.
Do not rewrite immutable archive evidence.

intent.problem: PR-first release metadata uses controlled origin/main refs, but supply-chain digest comparison treats those refs as literal values and blocks the candidate before merge.
constraints: Resolve only controlled origin refs or concrete SHA; never accept HEAD.
intent.rationale: All release evidence consumers must normalize the same controlled identity model before comparison so premerge metadata can be committed safely without weakening fail-closed checks.

rawUserRequest: When a release process issue is found, stop publication and fix the root process before continuing.

declaredIntent.summary: Normalize controlled symbolic release identity in supply-chain comparison while preserving fail-closed semantics.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Controlled origin identity is normalized before supply-chain digest comparison.
HEAD remains rejected as release identity evidence.
Publication remains out of scope and distribution remains fail-closed.
